display.purpose: Panel titles (Request Builder, Response, Schema). Tight tracking, semibold, sentence case.

body.purpose: Default UI body text. 14px, regular weight, 1.5 line-height.

label.purpose: Form field labels, button text, section labels. Medium weight, 13px.

mono.purpose: HTTP verbs, service paths, request bodies, response JSON, status codes, IDs. System mono stack with ligatures disabled.

shadows.name: drawer-ambient
shadows.purpose: Mobile sidebar drawer only. The single permitted shadow in the system.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (sidebar drawer, hover).

breakpoints.name: base
breakpoints.purpose: Mobile-first default. Sidebar is a drawer, content panels are tabs.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.purpose: Tablet and up. Sidebar becomes a persistent 320px panel, content panels split side-by-side.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.purpose: Desktop. Wider panels, max response panel capped at 60%.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Filled Honey CTA — Send Request, schema-sample fill. Use for the single primary action per screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--primary-strong); color: var(--primary-foreground); border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius); padding: 0.625rem 1rem; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover:not(:disabled) { background: var(--primary-strong-hover); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--background), 0 0 0 4px var(--ring); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action — method picker when not active, schema-fill, etc. Hairline border on Paper.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: var(--radius); padding: 0.625rem 1rem; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover:not(:disabled) { background: var(--secondary); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--background), 0 0 0 4px var(--ring); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Tertiary action — sidebar service rows when not selected, header icon buttons.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius); padding: 0.625rem 1rem; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); text-align: left; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover:not(:disabled) { background: var(--secondary); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--background), 0 0 0 4px var(--ring); }

components.name: Input
components.description: Form field — ID, auth token. Or textarea for JSON inputs. Hairline border, mono when JSON-shaped.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="Paste a JWT — sent as Authorization: Bearer …" />
components.css: .ds-input { background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: var(--radius); padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; font-family: ui-monospace, SF Mono, Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; line-height: 1.5; width: 100%; transition: border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px var(--ring); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Request Builder, Response, Schema containers. Paper bg, hairline border, no shadow.
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--card); color: var(--card-foreground); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: var(--radius); padding: 1.5rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; } .ds-card-header { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.375rem; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.25; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-card-body { flex: 1; min-height: 0; }

components.name: Method Chip
components.description: HTTP verb badge on service row and method picker. Uppercase mono. Active state on Burnt Amber.
components.css: .ds-chip-method { display: inline-block; padding: 2px 6px; background: var(--muted); color: var(--muted-foreground); border-radius: 2px; font-family: ui-monospace, SF Mono, Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; letter-spacing: 0; }

components.name: Status Badge (Success)
components.description: HTTP status code + status text. Mono. Pine bg + Pine text. Pairs with a plain-language summary sentence below.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 2px; font-family: ui-monospace, SF Mono, Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-badge-success { background: var(--success-bg); color: var(--success); }

components.name: Status Badge (Danger)
components.description: HTTP 4xx/5xx. Vermillion on a soft Vermillion wash.
components.css: .ds-badge-danger { background: oklch(from var(--destructive) l c h / 0.10); color: var(--destructive); }

components.name: Sidebar Service Row (Selected)
components.description: Selected service in the sidebar. Filled Burnt Amber bg, Bone text. Multi-line row: name + path (mono) + method chips.
components.css: .ds-svc-row { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; width: 100%; min-height: 44px; padding: 12px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius); background: transparent; color: var(--foreground); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; text-align: left; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-svc-row:hover:not(.ds-svc-row-selected) { background: var(--secondary); } .ds-svc-row-selected { background: var(--primary-strong); color: var(--primary-foreground); } .ds-svc-row-selected:hover { background: var(--primary-strong-hover); } .ds-svc-row:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--background), 0 0 0 4px var(--ring); } .ds-svc-name { font-weight: 500; } .ds-svc-path { font-family: ui-monospace, SF Mono, Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; opacity: 0.8; } .ds-svc-chip-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 4px; } .ds-chip-method-on-strong { background: oklch(from var(--primary-foreground) l c h / 0.15); color: var(--primary-foreground); }

narrative.overview: The Feathers Playground is a tool that disappears into the developer's workflow. Like a well-kept lab notebook, it earns its space by being precise, legible, and unobtrusive — the page is there to record the experiment, not to show off. Color is a marker for state, not decoration. Type is functional, with monospace reserved for the things that look right in monospace: HTTP verbs, service paths, request bodies, response JSON. The system rejects the default 2025 dev-tool look. No stock shadcn blue, no SaaS cream gradients, no Tailwind UI look-alike templates, no playful emoji empty states, no information-overload à la Postman. The chrome is quiet enough that a developer's attention stays on the request and the response — not on the tool.
keyCharacteristics: Terminal-adjacent vocabulary: Mono for verbs, methods, paths, JSON. Sharp typographic hierarchy. Not a costume, just familiar.
Restrained palette: Pure white paper, near-black ink with a brand undertone, a single honey accent reserved for action and selection. Aubergine accents typographic moments.
Flat by default: Depth comes from hairline borders and a single surface-card layer. No shadows at rest.
Earned density: Dense where developers work (method picker, headers, body, response); spacious where they read (status badges, errors, schema).
Honest state: Real HTTP status codes, real error messages, real auth flow. No masking.

rules.name: The Two Voices Rule
rules.body: Honey and Aubergine are the only chromatic colors. Honey speaks for action (a button to press, a state that matters). Aubergine speaks for reference (a link to follow, a value to read). Never both at once on the same element.

rules.name: The Rarity Doctrine
rules.body: The Honey accent covers ≤10% of any given screen. Its scarcity is what makes it meaningful. When in doubt, leave it out — Ink on Paper is enough.

rules.name: The Mono Discipline
rules.body: Mono is for code-shaped text only. Body prose, labels, and titles stay in Inter. Mixing them dilutes the terminal vocabulary.

rules.name: The No All-Caps Rule Except in Mono
rules.body: Display and label text use sentence case. The only all-caps text in the UI is mono — HTTP verbs, method chips — because that's what all-caps means in this system.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow is a signal that the surface is floating above the page — that means it's modal, drawer, or popover. If it isn't, it doesn't have a shadow.

rules.name: The Hairline Doctrine
rules.body: 1px borders separate surfaces. 2px borders are reserved for active tab indicators. No thicker colored border on any element.

dos: Do use Honey on action elements (filled buttons, active selections, focus rings) and nowhere else. The accent's rarity is the point.
Do use Aubergine for typographic moments (links, code callouts, secondary text accents) — never alongside Honey on the same element.
Do reserve mono for code-shaped content: HTTP verbs, service paths, JSON, IDs, status codes. Use Inter for everything else.
Do pair every status badge with a plain-language summary sentence below it. Color alone never carries the meaning.
Do enforce 44×44px touch targets on coarse pointers via @media (pointer: coarse) — already wired in globals.css.
Do anchor the input border to Input and the focus ring to Ring. The focus ring replaces the browser outline, never supplements it.
Do use the dark token set for night mode — the palette is rebuilt, not inverted.
donts: Don't use the stock shadcn blue. It is the explicit anti-reference and has no place here.
Don't use SaaS cream gradients, Notion pastel gradients, or any tinted near-white body background. The body stays pure white. Always.
Don't ship Postman-style information overload. Density is fine; chaos is not. One summary per panel, not three.
Don't use emoji as empty-state illustration. Empty states teach the next action in mono copy, not a smiling wrench.
Don't use Tailwind UI look-alike marketing templates. This is a tool, not a landing page.
Don't add a shadow to anything that isn't a modal, drawer, or popover. The flat-by-default rule is non-negotiable.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-l / border-r greater than 1px as a colored accent). Use full hairline borders, background tints, or leading glyphs.
Don't use opacity on hover states. Use an explicit darker token. Alpha creates unpredictable contrast.
Don't gate content visibility on a class-triggered transition. Reveal animations enhance an already-visible default; they don't create it.
